title: SLR-RFF-BRICK: Probabilistic sea-level rise from RFF-SP × FaIR × MimiBRICK
description: Reproducible pipeline for probabilistic sea-level rise projections, coupling the RFF-SP socio-economic / emissions ensemble (Rennert et al. 2022) with FaIR v2.2.4 (FaIR-calibrate v1.4.5 posterior, Smith et al. 2024; AR6-anchored update of v1.4.1) and MimiBRICK (post-PR#93 joint posterior from raddleverse/MimiBRICK.jl, Wong et al. 2026, which adds Frederikse 2020 GIS data to the calibration target set and matches Greenland mass-loss obs back to 1900). Importance-weighted against observed historical GMSL (Dangendorf et al. 2024) via Wong et al. 2026 AR(1) likelihood. Includes (i) the LHS-10k conditional-BRICK final ensemble (10,000 LHS triplets with Wong importance weights, ESS = 3,815 / 10,000 = 38.1%) for the headline projection band and pulse-marginal sensitivities, (ii) a Group-Sobol Hawkins-Sutton variance decomposition (importance-weighted, surrogate-based) of total and pulse-marginal SLR by source (emissions, climate response, BRICK posterior, internal variability), validated against an independent model-free 324,000-run balanced-factorial ANOVA cross-check (emissions/climate/internal shares agree to within ~2 percentage points), (iii) paired ±1-GtCO₂ and ±0.01-GtCO₂ pulse arms plus ±1-Tg and ±0.01-Tg CH₄ pulse arms for sign-flip and linearity tests.

creators.name: Sarofim, Marcus
creators.affiliation: NYU Marron Institute of Urban Management / Johns Hopkins EPCP
